summary: High‑energy Greek Theatre show with strong Terrapin > Stella and tight rhythm
blurb: The second set builds serious momentum, highlighted by a seamless Terrapin Station → Stella Blue sequence and powerful vocal harmonies. Standout moments include Brown Eyed Women, Desolation Row, and Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad. Overall the band delivers a high‑energy performance despite an uneven first set.
review:
On June 19 1987 at Berkeley’s Greek Theatre the Dead were firing on all cylinders. While the opening half of the first set meandered a bit, the band quickly found its stride, delivering crisp renditions of Brown Eyed Women and a punchy Desolation Row. The real magic unfolded in the second set: a soaring Terrapin Station that segued into a soulful Stella Blue—what reviewers called “the gravy on the biscuit”—and a rousing Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad that kept the audience dancing. Brent’s keyboards were hot, Jerry’s guitar sweet and incisive, Phil’s bass sat front‑center in the mix, and Bob added flamboyant vocal flourishes to Desolation Row. Even the encore, Black Muddy River, felt like a fitting close to an otherwise high‑energy night that captured the band at a sweet spot of their 1987 tour.

For listeners seeking the clearest window into this performance, the Walker‑Scotton matrix (gd1987-06-19.sbd.walker-scotton.miller.83765.sbeok.flac16) is the top pick: an excellent blend of audience ambience and soundboard clarity that lets every nuance—from Brent’s fiery keys to Jerry’s melodic leads—shine through. Whether you’re after the tight second‑set flow or just want a solid snapshot of late‑‘80s Dead, this show rewards repeated spins.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive (excellent matrix, solid matrix, excellent unknown source) while two are mixed (soundboard with uneven first set, audience with volume jumps). The majority of comments praise standout songs and high energy, yielding a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent matrix blend of audience and soundboard gives crisp, realistic sound that captures the band’s high‑energy performance.

key_highlights: Strong second‑set momentum with Terrapin Station → Stella Blue sequence
High‑energy playing throughout despite an unremarkable setlist on paper
Brent Mydland’s hot keyboards and Jerry Garcia’s sweet, incisive guitar tone
song_highlights: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a sweet‑toned guitar with incisive solos, especially on Stella Blue and Terrapin Station, and nails the energy throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Sits prominently in the front‑mix, providing a solid bass foundation that anchors the high‑energy set.
band_performance.Bob: Adds flamboyant vocal flourishes on Desolation Row and brings rock‑star intensity to that performance.
band_performance.Brent: Hot on keyboards, driving the jams with fiery energy and contributing strong melodic support.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
